# Interface Module

This module covers Go's interface system, which is the core of polymorphism in Go.

## Topics

- interface-basics: Interface definition, implementation, and usage
- interface-values: Interface value mechanism, nil interfaces
- empty-interface: Empty interface (any), type assertions, type switches
- interface-composition: Interface composition and embedding
- best-practices: Interface design principles, naming conventions, standard interfaces

## Standard Library Interfaces

The library/ subdirectory covers commonly used interfaces from Go's standard library and popular third-party packages:

- library/index: Overview and learning path for standard library interfaces
- library/io: io.Reader, io.Writer, io.Closer, and composed interfaces
- library/http: http.Handler, http.ResponseWriter, and HTTP server interfaces
- library/error: error interface, error wrapping, and error handling patterns
- library/sort: sort.Interface for custom sorting
- library/fmt: fmt.Stringer, fmt.GoStringer, and formatting interfaces
- library/context: context.Context interface and usage patterns
- library/gorm: GORM ORM interfaces including DB, Session, hooks, and plugins

## Key Concepts

Go interfaces are implicit - types implement interfaces without explicit declarations. This decouples interface definitions from implementations, making code more flexible and maintainable.

## Related Topics

- Methods (functions-methods/methods.mdx): Method receivers and interface implementation
- Struct types (data-types/struct.mdx): Custom types that implement interfaces
- Type system (fundamentals/type-system.mdx): Underlying type system
